questions.question: Which VLAN is used by default on a Cisco switch access port before configuration?
What is the primary purpose of creating VLANs in a campus network?
On an 802.1Q trunk, what happens to frames in the native VLAN by default?
Which command sets a switchport to carry multiple VLANs?
Which protocol dynamically negotiates trunk links on Cisco switches?
A user port must be placed in VLAN 10. Which interface command applies the VLAN to an access port?
A user port must be placed in VLAN 20. Which interface command applies the VLAN to an access port?
A user port must be placed in VLAN 30. Which interface command applies the VLAN to an access port?
A user port must be placed in VLAN 40. Which interface command applies the VLAN to an access port?
A user port must be placed in VLAN 50. Which interface command applies the VLAN to an access port?
A user port must be placed in VLAN 60. Which interface command applies the VLAN to an access port?
A user port must be placed in VLAN 70. Which interface command applies the VLAN to an access port?
A user port must be placed in VLAN 80. Which interface command applies the VLAN to an access port?
Two switches use an 802.1Q trunk. Which command changes the native VLAN to 99?
Two switches use an 802.1Q trunk. Which command changes the native VLAN to 999?
Two switches use an 802.1Q trunk. Which command changes the native VLAN to 4094?
Two switches use an 802.1Q trunk. Which command changes the native VLAN to 200?
Which issue is most associated with a native VLAN mismatch between trunk peers?
options: Untagged traffic can leak into the wrong VLAN

questions.question: What does the command 'switchport trunk allowed vlan 10,20,30' do?
options: Restricts trunk forwarding to VLANs 10, 20, and 30

questions.question: Which frame field carries VLAN tagging information on 802.1Q trunks?
options: Tag Protocol Identifier and Tag Control Information

questions.question: What is the best reason to disable DTP on an explicitly configured trunk?
Which command prevents a port from negotiating trunking via DTP?
What is the primary role of a voice VLAN on an access port?
options: Separate IP phone traffic from data traffic

questions.question: Which verification command shows VLAN-to-port assignments on a switch?
In a router-on-a-stick design, what is required on the switch link to the router?
options: An EtherChannel in PAgP desirable mode

questions.question: Which command enables an SVI for VLAN 20 with an IP address?
What does VTP primarily distribute between switches in the same VTP domain?
Which VTP mode can create, modify, and delete VLANs and advertise them?
Why should unused access ports be assigned to an unused VLAN and shutdown?
What is the main purpose of Spanning Tree Protocol?
Which switch becomes root bridge in STP by default?
options: The switch with the lowest bridge ID

questions.question: In STP, what is the bridge ID composed of?
Which STP port role forwards traffic toward the root bridge?
On a non-root switch segment, which port role forwards frames away from the segment toward hosts?
Which RSTP port role provides a backup path to the root bridge?
Which command is used to make a switch the root bridge for a VLAN in Cisco best-practice style?
What does PortFast do on an edge access port?
options: Transitions the port quickly to forwarding state

questions.question: Which feature shuts down a PortFast-enabled port if a BPDU is received?
What does Root Guard protect against?
options: An unexpected switch becoming root on that port

questions.question: Which STP timer defines how long a switch waits before changing topology information?
In classic 802.1D STP, what are the two transitional states before forwarding?
RSTP combines blocking/listening/disabled into which common state?
Which command verifies STP root information for all VLANs?
Using short path cost values, what is the typical STP cost of a 1 Gbps link?
Using short path cost values, what is the typical STP cost of a 100 Mbps link?
Using short path cost values, what is the typical STP cost of a 10 Mbps link?
Using short path cost values, what is the typical STP cost of a 10 Gbps link?
When two paths to the root have equal cost, what STP tie-breaker is considered next?
If bridge ID tie still exists, what is the next tiebreaker for root port election?
Which condition commonly causes topology change notifications in STP?
options: A forwarding port moving to blocking or link down/up

questions.question: Loop Guard is most useful on which type of STP ports?
options: Non-designated ports where BPDUs are expected

questions.question: What is the main benefit of EtherChannel?
options: Bundles multiple physical links into one logical link

questions.question: Which protocol is standards-based for negotiating EtherChannel?
Which EtherChannel protocol is Cisco proprietary?
Which LACP mode actively sends negotiation packets?
In LACP, which mode responds to LACP packets but does not initiate?
For a reliable EtherChannel, what must match across member interfaces?
options: Speed, duplex, VLAN/trunk settings, and allowed VLANs

questions.question: If one physical link in an EtherChannel fails, what happens?
options: Traffic continues over remaining links in the bundle

questions.question: Which command verifies EtherChannel summary status?
Which mode combination forms a PAgP channel?
options: desirable with auto (or desirable with desirable)

questions.question: Which LACP mode combination forms a channel?
Which command enters Port-Channel interface 1 configuration mode?
Which command enters Port-Channel interface 2 configuration mode?
Which command enters Port-Channel interface 5 configuration mode?
Which command enters Port-Channel interface 10 configuration mode?
Which command enters Port-Channel interface 20 configuration mode?
What is the function of CAPWAP in Cisco wireless architectures?
options: Carries control/data traffic between AP and WLC

questions.question: Which AP mode serves clients and forwards traffic through a WLC?
Which AP mode is designed to detect rogue APs and perform IDS functions rather than serve clients?
What is the key advantage of 5 GHz over 2.4 GHz in enterprise WLAN design?
options: More non-overlapping channels and typically less interference

questions.question: Which channel widths are commonly used in Wi-Fi design tradeoffs?
Which wireless security option is considered strongest for modern enterprise deployments?
What does 802.1X provide in WLAN access design?
options: Port-based network access control using authentication

questions.question: In wireless roaming, what best describes Layer 2 roaming?
options: Client always receives a new IP
Client moves APs within same subnet/VLAN without IP change

questions.question: Which design concern is directly related to co-channel interference (CCI)?
options: Too many nearby APs using the same channel

questions.question: What is RSSI primarily used to represent in WLAN troubleshooting?
options: Received signal strength at the client

questions.question: Which device typically maps SSIDs to VLANs in controller-based WLANs?
What is a common impact of using very wide channels (for example 80/160 MHz) in dense environments?
options: No need for transmit power tuning
Higher potential throughput but reduced channel reuse

questions.question: Which statement is true about SSID hiding as a security control?
options: It provides minimal real security by itself

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a guest WLAN in enterprise design?
options: Provide isolated Internet access for untrusted users

questions.question: Which mechanism is commonly used to centrally authenticate WLAN users?
In FlexConnect mode, what is one key branch-site benefit?
options: Local switching at the AP during WAN outages

questions.question: What is the role of a lightweight AP in centralized WLAN architecture?
options: Relies on WLC for most control-plane functions

questions.question: Which wireless issue is most likely when AP transmit power is set too high across many APs?
options: Sticky clients and increased co-channel contention

questions.question: What does SNR indicate in wireless design?
options: Signal quality relative to background noise

questions.question: Which AP deployment approach is most aligned with campus high-density best practice?
options: Single AP per floor regardless load
Planned channel/power design with validation survey

questions.question: What does switchport port-security maximum 2 enforce?
options: Allows up to two secure MAC addresses on the port

questions.question: Which port-security violation mode drops violating traffic but keeps port up and logs counters?
Which port-security mode places the port into err-disabled state on violation by default?
What is sticky MAC learning in Cisco port security?
options: Dynamically learned MACs are written into running-config as secure
MACs learned only from ARP replies

questions.question: Which command verifies port-security status and learned MACs?

options: Enable dynamic desirable on all edge ports
Disable unused ports and place them in an unused VLAN
Set all access ports as trunks
Use VLAN 1 for all user and management traffic
